doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_set_ui: convert an unsigned 64-bit integer to an MPFR at the requested precision, rounded per rnd, returning {value, ternary}. Specialisation of set_si for non-negative inputs only; sign is always +1. Class is 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget) for the same reason as set_si — the port is sub-millisecond in practice, but the runner enum lacks an 'arithmetic'-like tier this op fits cleanly into.
c_signature: int mpfr_set_ui(mpfr_t rop, unsigned long int op, mpfr_rnd_t rnd)
divergence_from_c: C takes a literal `unsigned long` (64 bits on the target platforms); TS takes a `bigint` and validates [0, ULONG_MAX] = [0, 2^64 - 1], throwing MPFRError('EPREC') on out-of-range. C mutates rop and returns the ternary; TS takes prec positionally and returns {value, ternary}. C sets +0 on n=0; TS matches.
refs: mpfr/src/set_ui.c L25–L29 — the C reference; trivial delegate to mpfr_set_ui_2exp.
mpfr/src/set_ui_2exp.c L26–L90 — the load-bearing implementation: zero short-circuit, leading-zero-count MSB-align, mpfr_round_raw on prec mismatch, sign assignment (always positive for unsigned).
src/ops/set_si.ts — signed sibling; this port is a direct specialisation with sign=+1 and widened range.
CLAUDE.md 'Hallucination-risk callouts' — ternary direction is sign of (rounded - exact); rounding-mode count is FIVE.
